textPlain: > Redox had a read-only ZFS driver but it was abandoned because of the monolithic nature of ZFS that created problems with the Redox microkernel design.Curious about the details behind those compatibility problems.

According to this https://www.redox-os.org/faq/
Looks like snapshots are planned.
Why? Why not simply adopt btrfs?

Well they’d have to write their own driver anyway for one. If they were going to take an existing design and write a new driver, ZFS would be the better choice by far. Much longer and broader operational history and much better documentation.
Why not use ext2 or fat16?
According to the bottom of their landing page [1], it's on the roadmap.[1] https://www.redox-os.org/
